title: build(deps): bump github.com/containers/storage from 1.45.5-0.20230326103843-b1216421c44b to 1.46.0
body:
Bumps [github.com/containers/storage](https://github.com/containers/storage) from 1.45.5-0.20230326103843-b1216421c44b to 1.46.0.
Release notes

Sourced from github.com/containers/storage's releases.

v1.46.0

What's Changed

  • Bump to v1.45.4 by @​rhatdan in containers/storage#1517
  • build(deps): bump github.com/klauspost/compress from 1.15.15 to 1.16.0 by @​dependabot in containers/storage#1521
  • build(deps): bump github.com/stretchr/testify from 1.8.1 to 1.8.2 by @​dependabot in containers/storage#1520
  • chunked: report the correct size by @​giuseppe in containers/storage#1523
  • fix(deps): update module golang.org/x/sys to v0.6.0 by @​renovate in containers/storage#1524
  • build(deps): bump github.com/containerd/stargz-snapshotter/estargz from 0.14.1 to 0.14.2 by @​dependabot in containers/storage#1527
  • Fix typos in error messages by @​nalind in containers/storage#1529
  • cirrus: refactor Ubuntu and Fedora test tasks by @​nalind in containers/storage#1530
  • build(deps): bump github.com/containerd/stargz-snapshotter/estargz from 0.14.2 to 0.14.3 by @​dependabot in containers/storage#1532
  • build(deps): bump github.com/Microsoft/hcsshim from 0.9.7 to 0.9.8 by @​dependabot in containers/storage#1537
  • fix(quota): added missed ClearQuota method to fix go-builds without cgo by @​distorhead in containers/storage#1538
  • fix(deps): update module github.com/vbatts/tar-split to v0.11.3 by @​renovate in containers/storage#1539
  • supportsOverlay: ensure that whiteout creation works by @​nalind in containers/storage#1545
  • [CI:DOCS] Document transient_store in man page by @​rhatdan in containers/storage#1544
  • Complete "pulling up" of images in updateNames() by @​nalind in containers/storage#1483
  • overlay: change error to debug log for unsupported fs by @​giuseppe in containers/storage#1547
  • fix(deps): update module github.com/opencontainers/runc to v1.1.5 [security] by @​renovate in containers/storage#1543
  • DriverTestSetQuota: Put() after Get() by @​nalind in containers/storage#1548
  • Drop nameLooksLikeID() by @​nalind in containers/storage#1550
  • Move to golang 1.18 and later by @​rhatdan in containers/storage#1522
  • overlay: disable native idmap with mount-program by @​giuseppe in containers/storage#1551
  • fix(deps): update module golang.org/x/sys to v0.7.0 by @​renovate in containers/storage#1552
  • Cirrus: Migrate Ubuntu to Debian CI VM Images by @​cevich in containers/storage#1535

New Contributors

  • @​distorhead made their first contribution in containers/storage#1538

Full Changelog: https://github.com/containers/storage/compare/v1.45.4...v1.46.0

Commits
  • See full diff in compare view

Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself)

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dependabot[bot]* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign lsm5 for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@giuseppe @nalind PTAL c/storage update is causing issues

In [libpod.storageService.CreateContainerStorage()](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/251d91699de4e9aaab53ab6fea262d4b6bdaae8e/libpod/storage.go#L167-L168), podman has been retrieving the ID mappings which were assigned to a new storage Container from the `options` structure that it passed in to the storage library's `CreateContainer()` call.
The newest version of the storage library stopped modifying fields in options structures that are passed to it, because sort of thing is often a source of hard-to-find bugs.
The information being sought is recorded in the `Container` struct that `CreateContainer()` returns, so we can find it there.  I've added a patch to the current PR to do so.
https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1483 is causing this break, I believe.
I think this is some how loosing the IDMAPPINGS?
I _think_ https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1560/ is going to fix the rest of these.

OK, I won't notify you again about this release, but will get in touch when a new version is available. If you'd rather skip all updates until the next major or minor version, let me know by commenting `@dependabot ignore this major version` or `@dependabot ignore this minor version`. You can also ignore all major, minor, or patch releases for a dependency by adding an [`ignore` condition](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/supply-chain-security/configuration-options-for-dependency-updates#ignore) with the desired `update_types` to your config file.

If you change your mind, just re-open this PR and I'll resolve any conflicts on it.
